sufi_quote: Whoever knows himself knows his Lord."
ayah_meaning: So surely with hardship comes ease. Surely with hardship comes ease."
story_teachings_placeholder: This story teaches us about faith and values in Islam.
zakat_hadith_quote: "The believer's shade on the Day of Resurrection will be his charity."
zakat_rules_sub: Learn about the fundamentals of Zakat in Islam
what_is_zakat_sub: Learn the fundamental meaning and importance of Zakat
who_must_pay_zakat_sub: Understand the conditions for Zakat obligation
nisab_hawl_sub: Understanding the minimum threshold and time period
assets_included_sub: What types of wealth are subject to Zakat
who_can_receive_sub: The eight categories of Zakat recipients
who_cannot_receive_sub: People who are not eligible for Zakat
purify_wealth_sub: Purify your wealth with ease and accuracy
above_nisab_msg: Your wealth is above the Nisab threshold. Zakat of 2.5% is obligatory on your total assets.
below_nisab_msg: Your wealth is below the Nisab threshold. Zakat is not obligatory at this time, but voluntary charity (Sadaqah) is always encouraged.
reminder_1: Zakat is due only if wealth has been in possession for one lunar year (Hawl)
reminder_2: Ensure gold and silver prices are updated to current market rates
reminder_3: Consult a scholar regarding jewelry worn regularly by women
reminder_4: Distribute Zakat to eligible recipients as defined in the Quran (9:60)
zakat_meaning_growth: The word "Zakat" means purification and growth. It purifies your wealth and helps it grow in blessings.
zakat_pillar: Zakat is one of the Five Pillars of Islam, making it an obligatory act of worship.
zakat_percent: Zakat is 2.5% of your accumulated wealth that has been in your possession for one lunar year (Hawl).
zakat_charity_poor: It is a form of charity ordained by Allah to help the poor and needy in the community.
zakat_obligation_justice: Paying Zakat is not just giving away money—it is fulfilling a divine obligation and ensuring social justice.
zakat_quran_verse: Allah says in the Quran: "Take from their wealth a charity to cleanse them and purify them." (9:103)
guidance_desc: For specific questions about your situation, consult with a knowledgeable Islamic scholar or imam in your community. They can provide personalized guidance based on authentic sources.
zakat_conditions_title: Every Muslim who meets the following conditions must pay Zakat:
cond_adult_sane: Must be an adult and sane
cond_free: Must be a free person (not a slave)
cond_nisab: Must possess wealth above the Nisab threshold
cond_nature: The wealth must be of a growing nature (cash, gold, silver, business assets, etc.)
exempt_notice: Children and insane persons are exempt, though guardians may pay on their behalf if they have substantial wealth.
nisab_reset_notice: If your wealth falls below Nisab during the year, the count resets when it reaches Nisab again.
zakat_recipients_title: Allah has defined 8 categories of people eligible to receive Zakat in Surah At-Tawbah (9:60):
recipient_poor: The Poor (Al-Fuqara) - Those with no income or means
recipient_needy: The Needy (Al-Masakin) - Those with insufficient income
recipient_admin: Zakat Administrators - Those appointed to collect and distribute Zakat
recipient_hearts: Those Whose Hearts Are to Be Reconciled - New Muslims or those inclined toward Islam
recipient_slaves: Freeing Slaves - To help free those in bondage
recipient_travelers: Travelers in Need (Ibn As-Sabil) - Stranded travelers without resources
recipient_debt: Those in Debt (Al-Gharimin) - People burdened with debt
recipient_cause: In the Cause of Allah (Fi Sabilillah) - Islamic projects and those striving in Allah's path
zakat_distribution_notice: Distribution can be to one or more categories depending on need and circumstance.
cannot_receive_zakat_title: The following people cannot receive Zakat:
cannot_family: Your immediate family: parents, grandparents, children, grandchildren, spouse
cannot_wealthy: The wealthy or those who possess wealth above Nisab
cannot_non_muslims_except: Non-Muslims (except those whose hearts are being reconciled to Islam)
cannot_prophet_family: Descendants of the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) - they are prohibited from accepting Zakat
cannot_able_bodied: People who are physically capable of earning but choose not to work
zakat_family_notice:
However, extended family like cousins, aunts, uncles, nephews, and nieces CAN receive Zakat if they meet the criteria.

Giving Zakat to eligible relatives is actually more rewarding as it combines charity and maintaining family ties.

Always verify the recipient's eligibility or give through trusted Islamic organizations.
nisab_desc: Nisab is the minimum amount of wealth a Muslim must possess before Zakat becomes obligatory.
silver_nisab_recommendation: Most scholars recommend using the Silver Nisab as it benefits more poor people.
hawl_meaning_lunar_year: Hawl means one full lunar year (354-355 days). Your wealth must remain above Nisab for this entire period.
wealth_fluctuation_notice: If wealth fluctuates but stays above Nisab at the start and end of the year, Zakat is still due.
price_change_notice: The Nisab value changes based on current gold/silver market prices, so check regularly.
assets_list_title: The following assets are included when calculating Zakat:
asset_cash: Cash in hand and in bank accounts
asset_loans: Money lent to others (expected to be returned)
non_zakat_notice: Personal items like your home, car, clothing, and furniture are NOT subject to Zakat.
jewelry_dispute_notice: Gold/silver jewelry regularly worn by women has a difference of opinion—consult a scholar.

cond_hawl: The wealth must be in possession for one full lunar year (Hawl)
